text.title: decision-tree-process Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for decision-tree-process as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Analysis Paralysis: taking too long to decide, requiring more data before any choice.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Set decision deadlines. Accept "good enough" for reversible (two-way door) decisions. Reserve full rigor for one-way doors.

failure-mode.description: HiPPO (Highest Paid Person's Opinion): deferring to seniority or authority rather than data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use the weighted matrix. Document dissenting opinions. Authority is not evidence.

failure-mode.description: Resume-Driven Development: choosing a technology for personal resume benefit rather than project fit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Focus on business needs. Document explicitly why the technology fits the problem, not why it is interesting.

failure-mode.description: Cargo Culting: copying an architecture decision from a well-known company without validating fit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Netflix/Uber/Airbnb patterns were built for their scale and team size. Validate that the same context applies before adopting.

failure-mode.description: Undocumented Decisions: deciding without writing an ADR.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Institutional knowledge is lost when team members leave. The decision will be re-litigated, possibly making the same mistake.

failure-mode.description: Premature Optimization: solving performance or scale problems that do not yet exist.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Start simple. Optimize when data supports it. Shopify's 2.8M-line monolith serves millions of merchants.
